method_atom: a stochastic model of a single driven particle that steps along a one-dimensional discrete track by cycling through an internal multi-state mechano-chemical loop, yielding an exact analytical dwell-time distribution per site, an average stepping rate obeying a Michaelis-Menten-like saturating law consistent with the general velocity formula for an unbranched cyclic process, and an exact force-velocity relation; the single-particle model is then extended to many such particles moving on the same track under hard-core steric exclusion, producing a driven-exclusion many-body process whose steady states are mapped as a phase diagram over externally tunable control parameters.
object_atom: ribosomes treated as molecular motors that translocate (with alternating stepping and pausing) along a messenger-RNA track while polymerizing a protein, including the case of many ribosomes crowding on the same mRNA.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is the cyclic-state stochastic stepper on a 1D track with exact dwell-time/velocity/force relations plus its multi-particle exclusion-process extension and phase diagram; what is domain-specific is that the particle is a ribosome and the track is an mRNA being translated into protein.
